name: Ring Wired Video Doorbell Pro (2nd Gen)

specs.Bird's Eye View: Yes — overhead map tracking visitor path
specs.Power: Wired only — 16–24 VAC / 24 VDC, 10–40 VA transformer required
specs.Night Vision: Color night vision (ambient light) + infrared (~15 ft range in darkness)
specs.Pre-Roll: 6 seconds colour pre-roll before motion event

whatWorks: 1536p Head-to-Toe HD+ video with 1:1 square ratio — packages on the ground and full-body views are visible where other doorbells cut them off. A practical improvement over standard 16:9 doorbell cameras.
Radar-based 3D motion detection significantly reduces false alerts compared to older PIR-based models — reviewers report near-zero false triggers for cars and pedestrians after tuning motion zones.
Color night vision using ambient porch or street light delivers noticeably better night footage than infrared-only competitors, though performance degrades in complete darkness.
Tight Alexa ecosystem integration — Echo speakers announce visitors aloud, Echo Show displays live feed automatically.
worthKnowing: Recording requires a Ring Protect subscription — without it, you get live view only. No saved clips, no person or package alerts. In Canada: Ring Solo $5.99/mo or $59.99/yr (one device); Ring Multi $14.99/mo or $149.99/yr (all devices). There is no local storage option.

Wired installation only — requires a 16–24V transformer with at least 40 VA. Many older Canadian homes have underpowered 16V/10VA transformers that cause connectivity and feature failures. Budget for potential transformer replacement.
People-only detection is unreliable — reviewers found it unable to consistently distinguish between humans and pets, triggering false alerts despite the advanced radar setup.
Night vision in complete darkness is limited to approximately 15 feet of usable range — fine detail beyond that goes soft. The color vision advantage applies only when ambient light is present.

commonComplaints.title: Night vision in complete darkness only…
commonComplaints.body: Night vision in complete darkness only reaches about 15 feet — fine detail beyond that goes muddy

commonComplaints.title: Old doorbell wiring compatibility is a…
commonComplaints.body: Old doorbell wiring compatibility is a real issue — underpowered transformers cause connectivity drops and missing alerts. Had to replace my transformer before it worked properly.

commonComplaints.title: Recording requires a paid subscription with…
commonComplaints.body: Recording requires a paid subscription with no local storage option — I feel held hostage by monthly fees just to access clips I've already captured

commonComplaints.title: People-only detection is unreliable — still…
commonComplaints.body: People-only detection is unreliable — still sends false alerts for my dog and even passing tree shadows

verdict: The Ring Wired Doorbell Pro 2 delivers the best video resolution and most accurate motion detection Ring has made — the 1:1 square FOV genuinely solves the 'where's my package' problem, and radar-based motion cuts false alerts meaningfully. The mandatory Ring Protect subscription ($5.99–$14.99/mo CAD) means your true cost climbs fast — factor that in before comparing it to the sticker price. Best suited for wired homes with adequate transformer output and existing Ring or Alexa ecosystems.

realBuyers.quote: This is a home security system, a personal front door concierge, and a delivering aide all in one.
After adjusting motion zones and sensitivity, I basically stopped getting false alerts from cars or street-level pedestrians entirely.
The 1:1 square view is a genuine improvement — I can actually see packages left on the step, which I couldn't with my old 16:9 doorbell.
Had to replace my doorbell transformer to get it working properly — the old 10VA one wasn't enough. Wish that was clearer in the listing.

sources.title: Ring Video Doorbell Pro 2 review — TechRadar
Ring Wired Video Doorbell Pro review — Tom's Guide

metaDescription: Ring Wired Doorbell Pro 2 review for Canada: 1536p video, radar motion detection, Bird's Eye View. Subscription required. Wiring requirements, pricing, and owner complaints.
introText: The Ring Wired Video Doorbell Pro (2nd Gen) is Ring's flagship wired doorbell, featuring 1536p Head-to-Toe video, radar-based 3D motion detection, and Bird's Eye View — with a mandatory Ring Protect subscription for video recording. Available on Amazon.ca (ASIN B086QMNXFQ) at $329 CAD.
